title: leptorumohra miqueliana var. fimbriata
intro: Leptorumohra miqueliana var. fimbriata is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its unique fronds and foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Leptorumohra miqueliana var. fimbriata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusate, with the fronds and rhizomes being used to create herbal preparations such as decoctions and tinctures.
